behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: A pure mock can miss real STT API response shape/diarization behavior (label fields, speaker-tag formatting, partial/streamed chunks). This boundary is at the real external speech-to-text call plus service wiring; no multi-step agentic pipeline is required to detect the core failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against a real speech-to-text API using a real multi-speaker recording with known speaker turns. Assert the transcription payload returned to the summarization stage still contains speaker identifiers per utterance and that final attributions reference those identifiers rather than unlabeled text.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM extraction quality from transcript content (who was assigned what). Deterministic mocks would hide misses in assignment detection/coverage. This is a single agentic transformation, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript samples with ground-truth task assignments per attendee, call the production LLM summarization endpoint, and verify each attendee with an assigned task appears in the action-items list at least once. Evaluate over repeated runs with acceptance thresholds to handle non-determinism.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is a semantic placement/classification requirement on LLM output. Mocks can validate schema but cannot expose real model behavior where decisions are omitted or misplaced. It is one model-output quality step.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with explicitly labeled decision statements, invoke the real summarization model, and assert those decisions appear in the structured `decisions` field and are not only present in free-form narrative fields. Run multiple trials and enforce minimum hit-rate.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans multiple sequential model-backed steps (segmenting, per-segment transcription/summarization, merge synthesis). Real failures emerge from cross-step interactions (ordering, context loss, merge quality) that lower-level tests can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Run a >60-minute real meeting audio through the production pipeline with real STT and LLM calls enabled. Assert: segmentation occurs at expected boundaries, segments are processed in order, intermediate outputs are chained, and final merged summary is single structured output with cross-segment consistency checks (no dropped attendees/tasks/decisions at boundaries).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is end-to-end operational integration with real external services (upload handling, API auth/timeouts/retries, payload formats, delivery). This can fail even when mocked tests pass. The assertion is completion, not model-quality scoring, so agentic level is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run in a production-like environment: upload a real meeting recording, perform real STT and summarization API calls, and verify a structured output artifact is delivered automatically within SLO and without operator steps or manual retries.
